structural_claim: Physical phenomena in both quantum and classical wave systems are fundamentally constrained by topology, with topological invariants protecting certain states against perturbations

supporting_results.structural_conclusion: Topological soliton solutions arise necessarily from the topology of the vacuum manifold in gauge theories with spontaneous symmetry breaking
Topologically distinct vacuum configurations lead to non-perturbative effects through tunneling between different topological sectors
Global topological properties of band structures determine the existence of edge states that are immune to backscattering from local perturbations
